name: Armenian Genocide and late Ottoman genocides of Armenians, Assyrians, and Greeks
summary: The Armenian Genocide and related late Ottoman genocides were state-organized campaigns of deportation, massacre, forced labor, starvation, disease, and expulsion directed mainly against Armenians, Assyrians/Syriac Christians, and Ottoman Greeks. The principal perpetrators were the CUP-led Ottoman state during World War I and, in the postwar phase, Turkish Nationalist/Kemalist forces and allied irregulars. A defensible death-toll range for the targeted Christian populations is about 1.20 million to 2.55 million, though direct and indirect deaths cannot usually be separated cleanly.

sides.name: Ottoman Empire / Committee of Union and Progress / Turkish National Movement

military_killed.notes: No reliable casualty count exists for Ottoman/Turkish forces killed specifically in genocide operations. Ottoman World War I all-front military dead/missing are often given as 771844, but that figure is not rolled into the genocide total.

breakdown.victims_group: Armenian civilians and disarmed Armenian men of fighting age
breakdown.notes: Direct killings were central to the genocide, but major sources usually combine direct killings with deaths from deportation, starvation, exposure, disease, and camp conditions.
Assyrian/Syriac deaths are generally reported as an aggregate Sayfo death toll rather than a direct-killing subtotal.

breakdown.victims_group: Ottoman Greeks, including Pontic and Anatolian Greeks
breakdown.notes: Greek death-toll sources combine direct killings with deportation, labor battalions, hunger, disease, and exposure.

civilians_killed_directly.notes: Direct civilian killings by Ottoman/CUP/Turkish Nationalist forces were extensive, but a clean all-victim direct-killing subtotal is not available.

deaths_from_actions.notes: Aggregate Armenian genocide deaths. USHMM gives 664000-1200000 for the main 1915-1916 genocide; broader historical ranges commonly extend to 1500000 for 1915-1923. Direct and indirect components are not cleanly separable.

deaths_from_actions.victims_group: Armenians in Syrian and Mesopotamian camps
deaths_from_actions.notes: Tatoyan reports that detailed contemporary estimates assigned about 400000 Armenian deaths in 1916 to concentration camps in Syria and Mesopotamia due to starvation, disease, and subsequent actions. This is a subcomponent of the Armenian aggregate, not an additional total.
Britannica says upwards of 250000 Assyrians were killed during World War I; 250000-300000 is a common range in Assyrian and secondary summaries.

deaths_from_actions.victims_group: Ottoman Greeks, including Pontic and Anatolian Greeks
deaths_from_actions.notes: Academic estimates for Ottoman Greek deaths in Anatolia during 1914-1922/1923 range roughly 289000-750000. Higher claims exist but are not used in the main roll-up.

deaths_from_actions.victims_group: Armenian refugees and civilians in the First Republic of Armenia
deaths_from_actions.notes: Some sources state that at least 200000 people in Armenia, mostly refugees, died from starvation or disease in 1918-1920 partly due to Turkish blockade and crop destruction. This is flagged separately and not added to the main roll-up because of possible overlap with Armenian survivor/refugee counts.

sides.name: Russian Empire / Armenian volunteer and Armenian National forces

military_killed.notes: No reliable military death figure was found that is specific to this genocide dataset rather than the wider Caucasus front and Armenian-Turkish wars.

breakdown.notes: Some summaries state that Russian and Armenian forces massacred as many as 60000 Muslims; the figure is contested and not used in the genocide roll-up.

civilians_killed_directly.notes: Armenian/Russian-associated forces committed revenge killings and massacres of Muslim civilians, but reliable aggregate numbers are lacking and politically contested.

sides.name: Kingdom of Greece / Greek occupation forces and associated bands
members: associated Armenian bands in some areas

military_killed.notes: Greek military deaths in 1919-1922 belong to the wider Greco-Turkish War and are not counted as part of the Ottoman genocides dataset.

breakdown.victims_group: Turkish/Muslim civilians in Yalova, Gemlik, and Izmit regions
breakdown.notes: The 1921 Inter-Allied Commission directly observed 28 bodies in villages it visited and found systematic destruction of Turkish villages, but stated that the fate of much of the population was unknown and that no accurate figures could be given.

civilians_killed_directly.notes: Greek occupation forces and associated bands committed serious atrocities against Turkish/Muslim civilians, but no reliable all-region aggregate death toll was found.

totals.notes: Grand totals roll up the targeted Christian-population genocide deaths only: Armenians 664000-1500000, Assyrians 250000-300000, and Ottoman Greeks 289000-750000. Military deaths and Muslim/Turkish civilian deaths in the wider wars are not included because reliable comparable aggregates specific to the named genocide conflict are unavailable.

key_atrocities.name: Armenian deportations and death marches
key_atrocities.notes: Aggregate Armenian genocide death range; includes direct massacres and deaths from deportation, starvation, exposure, disease, and camp conditions.

key_atrocities.name: Syrian and Mesopotamian concentration camps / Deir ez-Zor phase
key_atrocities.notes: Tatoyan reports about 400000 Armenian deaths in 1916 in concentration camps in Syria and Mesopotamia due to starvation, disease, and subsequent actions. This is a subcomponent of the Armenian genocide total.

key_atrocities.name: Assyrian Genocide / Sayfo
key_atrocities.perpetrator: Ottoman Empire, allied irregulars, and Kurdish tribal forces
key_atrocities.notes: Assyrian deaths are generally estimated at upwards of 250000, often 250000-300000.

key_atrocities.name: Ottoman Greek genocide, including Pontic Greek deportations and labor battalions
key_atrocities.perpetrator: Ottoman Empire / CUP and Turkish National Movement
key_atrocities.victims_group: Ottoman Greeks, including Pontic and Anatolian Greeks
key_atrocities.notes: Academic estimates for Ottoman Greek deaths across 1914-1922/1923 range roughly 289000-750000.

key_atrocities.name: Burning of Smyrna / İzmir
key_atrocities.perpetrator: Turkish Nationalist forces, responsibility disputed in some sources
key_atrocities.notes: Death estimates vary widely and responsibility is disputed in some accounts; not separately added to the roll-up because victims are already within broader Greek/Armenian postwar totals.

key_atrocities.name: Yalova-Gemlik-Izmit atrocities against Muslim civilians
key_atrocities.perpetrator: Greek occupation forces and Greek/Armenian bands
key_atrocities.notes: The Inter-Allied Commission directly observed 28 bodies and found systematic destruction of Turkish villages, but did not provide an accurate aggregate death toll.

uncertainty_notes: The principal uncertainty is that direct massacres and indirect deaths from deportation, starvation, disease, exposure, forced labor, and camp confinement are intertwined in the sources. Armenian figures are the best documented; Assyrian and Greek estimates are less demographically secure. Counter-atrocities against Muslim/Turkish civilians by Armenian/Russian and Greek forces are real but lack reliable aggregate estimates comparable to the Ottoman genocide figures and are therefore not rolled into the main genocide death toll.
